title: ononis natrix subsp. prostrata
intro: Ononis natrix subsp. prostrata, also known as the creeping restharrow, is a sub-species of the restharrow plant, a type of flowering plant that belongs to the legume family. The creeping restharrow has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to act as a diuretic and to treat kidney stones due to its high concentration of anthraquinone glycosides. The plant is also believed to have health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. In herbal preparations, the creeping restharrow is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
